transcript:
HCP: hi, thanks for coming in, i'm dr liam, can i ask how you've been feeling lately
Patient: yeah, not good honestly, just feeling really anxious all the time
HCP: that sounds tough, what triggers that anxiety for you
Patient: mostly work deadlines, i get this panic in my chest and can't focus on anything
HCP: and how are you sleeping with all this going on
Patient: i'm up until 3am worrying, then can't get back to sleep, just lying there
HCP: okay, do you notice any changes in how you talk or move around
Patient: i think i'm just talking fast and fidgeting, like i can't sit still
HCP: i see, and do you feel like you're seeing things that aren't there or hearing voices
Patient: no no, that's not it, i'm just really paranoid about people watching me
HCP: okay, do you feel you understand why we've got you here today
Patient: yeah i guess i know i need help, but i'm scared i'll just mess it up again

behaviour.evidence_span: i think i'm just talking fast and fidgeting, like i can't sit still

speech.evidence_span: i think i'm just talking fast and fidgeting, like i can't sit still

mood.evidence_span: not good honestly, just feeling really anxious all the time

thought.evidence_span: i'm just really paranoid about people watching me

insight.evidence_span: yeah i guess i know i need help, but i'm scared i'll just mess it up again

transcript:
HCP: hello, i'm dr lee, how are you doing today
Patient: hi doctor, honestly just terrible, feeling really anxious
HCP: can you tell me what's making you feel anxious
Patient: it's the noise, i can't stop hearing voices in my head, saying i'm worthless
HCP: what kind of voices are you hearing
Patient: they say i'm a failure, that everyone hates me, it's all in my head
HCP: and how is your sleep tonight
Patient: i can't sleep, my mind is racing, i just flip and flop
HCP: do you feel like you're losing touch with reality
Patient: no, i know it's just stress, i need to get my head on straight
HCP: thank you for being honest, we'll work on this together

thought.evidence_span: i can't stop hearing voices in my head, saying i'm worthless

cognition.evidence_span: i can't sleep, my mind is racing

insight.text: good insight, attributes symptoms to stress
insight.evidence_span: i know it's just stress, i need to get my head on straight

transcript:
HCP: hello, thanks for coming in, how are you feeling today
Patient: okay, a bit better than last week, thanks for asking
HCP: good to hear, what has been going on for you
Patient: i've been really anxious about work, can't stop worrying about mistakes
HCP: that sounds tough, tell me more about that anxiety
Patient: my heart races and i get sweaty palms every time i think about the meeting
HCP: and how is your sleep and appetite
Patient: sleep is okay, but i'm not hungry much lately
HCP: i notice you're talking quite fast, is that how you feel
Patient: yeah, i feel like my brain is buzzing non-stop
HCP: do you feel this is related to your usual mood
Patient: i don't really know, i just feel on edge all the time

transcript:
HCP: hi, good morning, can you tell me what brings you in today
Patient: yeah sure, i've been feeling really anxious lately, like i'm always waiting for something bad to happen
HCP: that sounds tough, and how is your sleep going
Patient: i can't turn my brain off at night, i just lie there worrying about bills and work, haven't slept in days
HCP: and have you noticed any changes in your appetite or energy levels
Patient: yeah, i've lost weight, i don't have the energy to get out of bed sometimes
HCP: do you feel like you're losing control or having strange thoughts
Patient: no, not strange thoughts, just constant worry and feeling on edge
HCP: do you think this is related to stress or something else
Patient: i think it's the stress at work, but i'm not sure if it will pass

mood.evidence_span: feeling really anxious lately, like i'm always waiting for something bad to happen

cognition.evidence_span: i can't turn my brain off at night, i just lie there worrying about bills and work, haven't slept in days

insight.text: partial insight - attributes to work stress
insight.evidence_span: i think it's the stress at work, but i'm not sure if it will pass

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for popping in, how are you doing today
Patient: yeah, not bad, just tired honestly
HCP: okay, tell me a bit about that tiredness
Patient: it's been going on for ages, i can't seem to focus on work
HCP: that sounds really draining, and how has your sleep been
Patient: terrible, i wake up at 3am and just stare at the ceiling
HCP: anything else bothering you recently
Patient: yeah, my mum keeps calling, she thinks i'm ignoring her
HCP: how does that make you feel
Patient: guilty, really guilty, but i don't know how to talk to her
HCP: and do you feel like you're hearing voices or seeing things
Patient: no, everything seems pretty normal, just really anxious
HCP: okay, so to sum up, it's the tiredness, the guilt with your mum, and the anxiety
Patient: yeah, that about covers it

thought.evidence_span: everything seems pretty normal, just really anxious

cognition.evidence_span: i can't seem to focus on work

transcript:
HCP: hello, thanks for seeing me today, how have you been since last week
Patient: honestly it's been a nightmare, i've been feeling really anxious all the time
HCP: what triggers that anxiety for you
Patient: mostly my job, i keep worrying i'll make a mistake and lose my position
HCP: and the second issue you mentioned, how are things with your partner
Patient: we haven't spoken in days, i'm so paranoid that they're plotting against me
HCP: paranoia is a big concern, can you describe what that feels like
Patient: i feel like people are watching me, listening to my phone conversations
HCP: and does that make you feel unsafe or angry
Patient: yeah, i get really irritable and snap at everyone when i feel threatened

behaviour.evidence_span: i get really irritable and snap at everyone when i feel threatened

mood.evidence_span: i've been feeling really anxious all the time

thought.text: persecutory delusions, fear of being watched
thought.evidence_span: i feel like people are watching me, listening to my phone conversations

cognition.evidence_span: mostly my job, i keep worrying i'll make a mistake and lose my position

insight.evidence_span: i'm so paranoid that they're plotting against me

transcript:
HCP: hi, thanks for making the appointment, what brings you in today
Patient: well, i've been having these panic attacks again, like out of nowhere
HCP: okay, can you describe what happens during one of them
Patient: my heart starts pounding, i feel like i'm going to die, it's so scary
HCP: and how long does that last for
Patient: usually about twenty minutes, then i just collapse on the floor
HCP: have you noticed any changes in your sleep or appetite recently
Patient: yeah, i'm barely sleeping at night, just tossing and turning
HCP: do you feel anxious most of the time between the attacks
Patient: yeah, i'm on edge all the time, checking the news constantly for disasters
HCP: do you think this is related to stress at work
Patient: probably, i've been overwhelmed with deadlines lately
HCP: do you understand why you're here today
Patient: yeah, i need medication, i guess, but i'm scared it won't help

behaviour.evidence_span: checking the news constantly for disasters

mood.evidence_span: i'm on edge all the time

thought.text: fear of dying, catastrophic misinterpretation of physical symptoms
thought.evidence_span: i feel like i'm going to die

insight.text: partial insight — acknowledges need for medication but fears efficacy
insight.evidence_span: i need medication, i guess, but i'm scared it won't help

transcript:
HCP: hi, good to see you, can you tell me what brings you here today
Patient: yeah sure, i've been feeling really anxious lately, like i can't breathe properly
HCP: sounds tough, how long has this been going on
Patient: about three weeks, started after my dog passed away, i just cry all the time
HCP: that's very hard to cope with, and what about your sleep
Patient: i can't sleep at night, i toss and turn, my mind is so loud
HCP: are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or others
Patient: no no, just feel really empty and hopeless about everything
HCP: do you feel you understand why you're seeing us today
Patient: yeah i know i'm depressed but i don't think meds will help

transcript:
HCP: hello, i'm dr chen, what brings you in today
Patient: well, i'm really anxious, i can't stop shaking
HCP: where do you feel that shaking
Patient: mostly in my hands and legs, it's been going on for a few days
HCP: and is there anything else troubling you
Patient: yes, i've lost my appetite and i feel like i'm constantly on edge
HCP: have you noticed any changes in your sleep or concentration
Patient: i can't sleep, my mind is just jumping from one thing to another
HCP: and do you feel guilty or worthless about this
Patient: sometimes, i worry i'm just broken and can't fix it

thought.evidence_span: my mind is just jumping from one thing to another

transcript:
HCP: hello, i'm dr lee, psych registrar here, thanks for meeting with me today
Patient: hi, yeah no worries, thanks for seeing me
HCP: so what brings you in today, what's been going on
Patient: i just can't stop worrying, it's been really hard lately
HCP: can you tell me more about that worry, what is it about
Patient: i keep thinking about every little thing going wrong, like i'll forget something important or mess up my job
HCP: sounds like your mind is very busy, how has that been affecting your sleep
Patient: pretty badly, i toss and turn, i just can't shut my brain off
HCP: and how has your mood been over the last few weeks
Patient: just terrible, i feel so anxious all the time, like i can't relax
HCP: do you feel like you understand why you're here today
Patient: yeah i think so, i just need help calming down, but i'm scared it won't stick
HCP: that sounds tough, let's work through this together
Patient: thanks, i appreciate it
HCP: are you seeing any other changes in how you act or feel
Patient: yeah i feel pretty on edge, twitchy, can't sit still

behaviour.evidence_span: yeah i feel pretty on edge, twitchy, can't sit still

mood.evidence_span: just terrible, i feel so anxious all the time, like i can't relax

thought.text: excessive worry about making mistakes, rumination
thought.evidence_span: i keep thinking about every little thing going wrong, like i'll forget something important or mess up my job

cognition.evidence_span: i just can't shut my brain off
